key_themes: AI-driven enterprise software earnings beats (Twilio, Atlassian, Five9)
Apple anchors tech rally with Q2 earnings beat, S&P 500 and Nasdaq hit fresh all-time highs
Consumer spending caution weighs on select names (Clorox cuts guidance, Roblox slashes outlook)

stocks.reason: Fiscal Q2 2026 earnings and revenue beat; better-than-expected Q3 revenue outlook; stock rose 3%+ powering S&P 500 and Nasdaq to new record closes

Q1 2026 earnings beat with EPS of $5.54 vs $4.63 expected, revenue $17.4B vs $16.49B est; record $62.7B backlog; AI data center power demand driving double-digit stock surge; BofA and Bernstein raise price targets
Q1 2026 beat with EPS $1.50 vs $1.14 prior year, revenue up 20% YoY to $1.41B; raised full-year guidance 14-15% revenue growth; AI voice demand cited; stock surged 20%+ premarket
FQ3 2026 beat with EPS $1.75 vs $0.97 prior year, revenue up 32% YoY to $1.79B; cloud and data center growth on AI adoption; stock jumped 25% premarket
Q1 2026 beat with revenue up 69% YoY to $663M (vs $609M est) and EPS $1.01 (vs $0.56 est); CEO highlighted AI data licensing advantage; stock jumped 15-16% premarket
Slashed full-year 2026 bookings guidance to $5.87B-$6.14B from $6.02B-$6.29B; child-protection safeguards impacting user numbers; stock tumbled 17-24% premarket; BofA downgraded to neutral from buy
Q1 2026 revenue of $1.25B beat expected $1.20B; raised guidance; stock rose 7-9% on the results
Q1 2026 EPS of $0.76 beat $0.69 estimate; revenue of $305M topped expectations; raised full-year outlook; stock soared ~30% in morning session on AI contact-center demand

Q1 2026 revenue of $400M beat estimates by 54%; EU approved mCOMBRIAX (world's first flu+COVID combo vaccine); reiterated full-year guidance; stock up ~8% premarket despite $1.3B net loss from litigation settlement
S&P 500 inclusion announced, joining the index effective May 7, 2026; stock popped 11% on the news
Cut full-year profit outlook citing consumer selectivity amid higher gas prices and spending pressure; stock tumbled 9%
Stock advanced 4.04% on continued momentum heading into its own earnings report; strong sector sentiment from broader e-commerce and software beats
